pi_items.metadata_value: Root Length / Crown Height ratio expected for maxillary central incisor
pi_items.verified_value: Published R/C ratio for maxillary central incisors is 1.1 ± 0.2; with defaults (8.0-14.0 crown, 10.0-20.0 root), ratio range would be 0.71-2.50, much broader than anatomy supports
pi_items.source: Dental anatomy literature and verifier.py ratio calculation (line 99)

privileged_info_summary: The task measures the Crown-to-Root ratio of the Maxillary Right Central Incisor (Tooth #8), with Crown Height defined as Incisal Edge to CEJ and Root Length as CEJ to Apex. Anatomically, maxillary central incisors have crown heights of 8.6-13.8 mm (mean 10.9 mm) and root lengths of 6.7-16.5 mm (mean 11.9-14.1 mm), with a typical R/C ratio of 1.1 ± 0.2. The verifier's default measurement ranges (crown 8.0-14.0 mm, root 10.0-20.0 mm) are reasonably calibrated to clinical tolerances, though the maximum root length of 20.0 mm exceeds published anatomical limits. The task.json file itself is malformed and incomplete, missing the metadata section and JSON closing braces.
